name: Bix Beiderbecke
abstract: Leon Bismark "Bix" Beiderbecke ( BY-dər-bek; March 10, 1903 – August 6, 1931) was an American jazz cornetist, pianist and composer. Beiderbecke was one of the most influential jazz soloists of the 1920s, a cornet player noted for an inventive lyrical approach and purity of tone, with such clarity of sound that one contemporary famously d…

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Bix Beiderbecke in the context of other musicians (Sharky, the Wolverines) and historical facts, stating 'I do think that in some respects that maybe the Rhythm Kings was some kind of an improvement... Bix came along and did change things'. There is no evidence of a persona

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Bix Beiderbecke only as part of a narrative about correspondence and historical knowledge held by others (e.g., 'knew Bix [Beiderbecke] and everything', 'knew things about King Oliver and about Bix Beiderbecke'). The interviewee himself does not claim a personal relationship
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Geoff Bull
about_quotes.evidence: I got a Bix record. And I'd read, you know, we used to read a little bit of books and that and then, and there's, you know, all these glorious mysteries that shroud characters like Bix because he was a big hard drinker and died young and all this sort of business.

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Robinson
about_quotes.evidence: He played in the ‘20s on all those great records with Bix Beiderbecke... Randy Sandke does a lot of Bix’s music... I get to sort of be the Trumbauer-Rollini amalgam in those concerts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Robinson interviewed by Monk Rowe,Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: I have an album, a vinyl album of Bucky Pizzarelli with a group of other guitar players doing arrangements of Bix Beiderbecke’s piano pieces, “In a Mist.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Lyde
about_quotes.evidence: He said that, uh, Ravel and Tabusi were Bix's favorite, uh, composers and he was stashing up every Ravel and Tabusi disc he could lay his hands on.
